interactions.summary: Dugin answers Jiang by defining the catechon as an Orthodox Christian and state-linked restrainer of Antichrist; Russia inherits the Byzantine/Roman role and must never side with evil even if evil is necessary in God's plan.
The host asks whether the Russian empire is the catechon and whether America can choose a better path if it is becoming disillusioned with war.
Dugin says Orthodox doctrine rejects predestination and affirms human freedom: Americans can accept or reject the Antichrist role, and he once saw Trump voters as signs of reversal.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization labels are not reliable in this multi-speaker interview; speaker attribution is inferred from conversational role and named address.

claims.claim: Dugin defines the catechon as the sacred Christian Orthodox empire/state function that delays and fights Antichrist rather than accelerating the second coming.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says the moral rule is never to side with evil, even if evil is somehow necessary in God's plan.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin reads Epstein files as religious confirmation that the modern West is Antichrist rather than merely a sexual scandal.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says there is no predestination: the human soul remains free to accept or reject the role of Antichrist.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says Trump's second-term campaign initially looked to Russians like a possible American reversal against post-human liberal globalism.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

signature_moments.moment: Dugin's moral answer is simple: never side with evil, even if evil has a place in God's plan.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Never side with the evil. Never.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the moral heart of his catechon answer to Jiang.

signature_moments.moment: Epstein becomes, for Dugin, not scandal but eschatological proof.
signature_moments.source_phrase: much more than just sexual scandal
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains why the interview keeps returning to Epstein as a theological sign.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The philosophical temptation to accelerate Antichrist in order to hasten the second coming, which Dugin morally rejects.
The duty to resist Antichrist until the last breath, even unto death.

uncertainty_notes.note: Transcript uses “catacomb/cataconic” for catechon/catechonic throughout; preserve source refs but normalize carefully in public prose.
